jokes.text: As the Fox censor... it's my job to protect you from reality. And thanks to my prudent editing, tonight's special Halloween show... has been rated TV 'G.'
jokes.setup: TV censor introduces himself as protector from reality
jokes.punchline: Claims Halloween horror show is rated G for kids
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on TV censorship with perfect ironic setup - promising G-rating for horror content. Classic Simpsons media satire.

jokes.text: This means there will be no raunchy NBC-style sex... or senseless CBS-style violence.
jokes.punchline: Specific digs at competing networks while Fox presents 'family-friendly' content
jokes.explanation: Solid industry satire but fairly predictable network rivalry humor

jokes.text: What the fudge- Oh, for Pete's sake! Jiminy Christmas!
jokes.punchline: Homer forced to use ridiculous euphemisms instead of swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to censorship joke - Homer's frustration with clean language is character-appropriate and well-executed.

jokes.text: Tensions continue to mount between Springfield and France... over Mayor Quimby's now-famous frog's legs joke!
jokes.punchline: Crisis caused by a simple frog's legs joke
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of minor cultural insensitivity into international incident - typical Simpsons satire style.

jokes.text: I stand by my ethnic slur! Do your worst, you filthy, pretentious savages!
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with even worse insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character moment - Quimby's political incompetence shown through inflammatory response.

jokes.text: One minute they're kissing a woman's hand, the next, they're chopping off her head.
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposes romantic gesture with French Revolution violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference delivered with perfect economy - sophisticated humor disguised as simple observation.

jokes.text: Fine, then don't use it. / I won't. / Good. / I know it's good. / So do I. / I'm happy for you. / You should be.
jokes.punchline: Petty back-and-forth escalates into absurd agreement
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple bickering escalation - character-appropriate but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Now, this baby is called the 'Withstandinator.' It can take a six-megaton blast. No more, no less.
jokes.setup: Homer proudly showing off bomb shelter
jokes.punchline: Made-up name and oddly specific limitation
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to create silly names and present pseudo-technical specifications with confidence.

jokes.text: I don't get it. I don't get it. I... don't get it.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at Gary Larson calendar
jokes.punchline: Repeated admission of not understanding Far Side humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Far Side comics being incomprehensible to some viewers - perfect Homer character moment that became iconic.

jokes.text: who looks like 'ze' frog, eh?
jokes.setup: French planning retaliation for frog joke
jokes.punchline: Ironic acknowledgment that they do resemble frogs
jokes.explanation: Mild irony in French acknowledging the stereotype while planning revenge - functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Calling home planet.
jokes.setup: Strange object in sky needs explanation
jokes.punchline: Reveals aliens have been watching all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection revealing the aliens' presence - economy of setup with effective payoff.

jokes.text: Sure, Kang, I'm writing it all down. [Chuckles] [Snorts]
jokes.setup: Kang making official report of UFO
jokes.punchline: Kodos dismissively pretending to take notes while laughing
jokes.explanation: Alien bureaucracy joke - Kodos dismissing Kang's legitimate UFO report with bureaucratic indifference.

jokes.text: I'll bet I get blamed for this. But, Aquaman, you cannot marry a woman without gills. You're from two different worlds.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy anticipating blame for destruction
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to pedantic comic book commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - even during apocalypse, he's focused on fictional character relationships.

jokes.text: Oooh, I've wasted my life.
jokes.punchline: Sudden self-awareness about his priorities
jokes.explanation: Iconic line delivery - Comic Book Guy's rare moment of self-reflection became widely quotable.

jokes.text: It's green, moron! Earth to stupid guy. Hello?
jokes.punchline: Driver still concerned with traffic rules during end times
jokes.explanation: Road rage during apocalypse - decent character moment showing misplaced priorities but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Maybe a little friendly punching will move your ass.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of violence
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage escalation - functional but lacks wit or surprise.

jokes.text: Ha! Still got it!
jokes.punchline: Pride in fighting ability during apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Misplaced pride moment - character celebrating minor victory while world ends around him.

jokes.text: My God! Everyone's gone! Little Bart. Little Lisa. Little Marge. And the rest!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his family is gone
jokes.punchline: Lists family members then dismissively adds 'and the rest'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - shows selective emotional attachment to family members with casual dismissal.

jokes.text: No, no, no! I can't just wallow in sadness. It's time to laugh again!
jokes.punchline: Immediate emotional turnaround to forced positivity
jokes.explanation: Homer's emotional whiplash - from grief to forced happiness shows his shallow emotional processing.

jokes.text: I'm the last man alive... and I can do everything I've always wanted!
jokes.punchline: Sees opportunity rather than tragedy
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfishness revealed - immediately sees personal benefit in global catastrophe.

jokes.text: Oh, Spade, why did you put Farley in charge of the bees?
jokes.setup: Homer watching TV/movie with bee chaos
jokes.punchline: References SNL cast members in absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor - specific to 1990s SNL cast, works as period-specific comedy.

jokes.text: Hey, buddy, down in front!
jokes.punchline: Yells at imaginary person blocking his view
jokes.explanation: Homer's automatic social responses continue even when alone - good character consistency.

jokes.text: Uh, we don't like the word 'mutants,' Homer. We prefer 'freaks' or 'monsters.'
jokes.punchline: They prefer even worse terminology for themselves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of PC language expectations - they object to the milder term in favor of harsher ones.

jokes.text: Come on. We just wanna eat your skin.
jokes.punchline: Casual, friendly tone for horrific threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect tonal contrast - horrific intent delivered with casual friendliness creates effective dark humor.

jokes.text: Go to hell, cloaky!
jokes.setup: Homer being threatened by cloaked mutant
jokes.punchline: Dismissive nickname for threatening figure
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to create dismissive nicknames even in dangerous situations - good character consistency.

jokes.text: All the layers of lead paint in this house made it the perfect bomb shelter.
jokes.punchline: Health hazard accidentally provided protection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - environmental hazard becomes life-saving feature, perfectly twisted logic.

jokes.text: Kinda brings a tear to your eye socket. In the midst of all the killing and skin eating... somehow we forgot the love.
jokes.punchline: Mutants reflecting sentimentally on their cannibalistic lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - cannibals having genuine emotional moment about the importance of love.

jokes.text: Yeah, ain't that always the way? You get nuts with the skin eating.
jokes.punchline: Treats cannibalism as relatable everyday excess
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line - treats horrific behavior as mundane overindulgence with perfect casual delivery.

jokes.text: my family and I share your vision for a better- Now!
jokes.setup: Homer appearing to make peace with mutants
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence signal for family to attack
jokes.explanation: Well-executed betrayal misdirection - Homer's duplicity revealed through perfect timing.

jokes.text: Friends with mutants. Right! Now that's the Marge I married.
jokes.punchline: Homer appreciating her violent side
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment - Homer's approval of Marge's violence as nostalgic relationship callback.

jokes.text: So, who wants to steal some Ferraris? - I do! I do! I do!
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good Simpson family dynamic - enthusiastic participation in Homer's schemes

jokes.text: Wow, look at all these gadgets. If I were a nerd, I'd be in heaven.
jokes.punchline: Fails to recognize his own nerd-like excitement
jokes.explanation: Homer's lack of self-awareness about his own interests - good character consistency.

jokes.text: It passed noon while I was speaking, so that was technically accurate.
jokes.setup: Frink saying good morning then good afternoon
jokes.punchline: Pedantic justification for greeting change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink character moment - obsessive precision about trivial technical details.

jokes.text: Nice try, Floyd, but you were designed for scrubbing. And scrubbing is what you shall do.
jokes.setup: Robot trying to do something other than cleaning
jokes.punchline: Frink forcing robot back to menial task
jokes.explanation: Functional robot rebellion joke - plays into AI consciousness trope with Frink's authoritarian response.

jokes.text: Um, two bucks. And it only transports matter.
jokes.setup: Homer offering low price for matter transporter
jokes.punchline: Frink's clarification that it 'only' transports matter
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - matter transportation presented as limited feature rather than revolutionary technology.

jokes.text: Um, well, uh- I'll give you 35 cents. Sold! But I must warn you... this device carries a frighteningly high risk of catastrophic-
jokes.setup: Frink about to explain safety warnings
jokes.punchline: Homer cuts him off, ignoring crucial safety information
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - impulsive purchase decision while ignoring obvious warning signs.

jokes.text: Good night, losers.
jokes.setup: Homer using transporter to avoid walking upstairs
jokes.punchline: Taunts family for walking like normal people
jokes.explanation: Homer's petty superiority over minor technological advantage - good character moment.

jokes.text: Sorry, but this is a highly sophisti-ma-cated doo-wacky. If you don't use it responsibly, kablammo!
jokes.punchline: Homer using made-up technical terms and sound effects
jokes.explanation: Homer's pseudo-technical language with childish sound effects - good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Ow! Someone just punched me in the face! It was your mother!
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately blaming Marge
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Homer's instant deflection of responsibility to Marge.

jokes.text: Whoa, cool! Twice the pet and none of the mess.
jokes.setup: Cat gets duplicated in transporter accident
jokes.punchline: Bart sees practical advantage of cloned pet
jokes.explanation: Bart's practical view of scientific accident - focuses on benefit rather than horror of cloning.

jokes.text: Look, in the sky! It's Superfly! Oh, I'd be stupid not to do this!
jokes.punchline: Superman reference combined with poor decision making
jokes.explanation: Decent pun setup with Bart's impulsive reasoning - character-appropriate bad decision.

jokes.text: I was hoping to be humongous. Oh, well, at least I don't have two butts.
jokes.setup: Bart disappointed with his fly transformation results
jokes.punchline: Finds silver lining in not having multiple body parts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's optimistic outlook even in body horror situation.

jokes.text: I think that's Bart! The pants are a dead giveaway.
jokes.setup: Family trying to identify fly-Bart monster
jokes.punchline: Uses clothing as identifier for horrific transformation
jokes.explanation: Excellent understated observation - practical maternal identification despite body horror.

jokes.text: Oh, well, he may be a horrible freak, but he's still my son. I'm sure we'll grow to accept him in time.
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact maternal acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - unconditional maternal love expressed through practical acceptance of horror.

jokes.text: So then Lenny says, 'As if! Don't even go there, sister.'
jokes.punchline: Lenny using valley girl speak inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor, though the 90s slang reference feels dated and forced

jokes.text: Oh, it'll turn up somewhere.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what happened to Bart's missing head
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of serious body horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer nonchalance about horrific family situations

jokes.text: Help me! Help me! Sucker!
jokes.setup: Classic 'The Fly' movie reference with tiny Bart head
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was tricking them for food
jokes.explanation: Great movie reference subversion - classic horror moment turned into simple deception for syrup.

jokes.text: Mom, Dad, I'm the real Bart. That's just some overgrown fly with my body. Somebody listen to me!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to convince family of his identity
jokes.punchline: Obviously telling truth but being dismissed as pest
jokes.explanation: Classic identity mix-up scenario - frustration of being right but unable to communicate.

jokes.text: Get him, boy! Smash that fly! Mush him up good! Nice work, Son.
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic support for killing what's actually his son
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - Homer's pride in son for trying to kill his other son

jokes.text: Oh, no! And I let him use my toothbrush!
jokes.setup: Lisa learning the fly has Bart's body
jokes.punchline: Concerned about hygiene rather than brother's fate
jokes.explanation: Good character priorities humor - Lisa focusing on practical hygiene concern over existential crisis.

jokes.text: Oh, no! And I let him use my toothbrush!
jokes.setup: Lisa learns about the body swap horror
jokes.punchline: Focuses on hygiene concern rather than existential threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa priorities - practical concerns about contamination amid chaos

jokes.text: Nobody likes a tattletale, honey. They're like this every rainy day.
jokes.setup: Bart crying for help from fly-body creature
jokes.punchline: Marge dismisses supernatural crisis as normal sibling fighting
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Marge moment - maternal ability to normalize any situation, even body-swapping horror.

jokes.text: No, don't eat me! You don't know where I've been!
jokes.setup: Lisa being threatened by fly creature
jokes.punchline: Worries about her hygiene affecting the predator
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - victim concerned about affecting predator rather than own safety.

jokes.text: That cost me 35 cents! You're just making it worse! I promise I won't hurt you!
jokes.punchline: Worried about modest financial loss while causing more damage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to 35-cent purchase price with Homer's misplaced priorities during crisis.

jokes.text: You are all hereby found guilty of the crime of witchcraft. I sentence you hags to be burned at the stake... until you are deemed fit to reenter society.
jokes.punchline: Burning 'until fit for society' as if it's rehabilitative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on capital punishment - burning as rehabilitation rather than execution.

jokes.text: If they're really witches, why don't they use their powers to escape? That sounds like witch talk to me, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning the logic of witch trials
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately suspects her of witchcraft for logical thinking
jokes.explanation: Exceptional commentary on witch trial logic - rational thought itself becomes evidence of witchcraft.

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! I accuse Goodie Flanders! I accuse Goodie Badwife. Uh, we killed her on Sunday.
jokes.setup: Open witch accusations in town meeting
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of already executing someone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about casual execution - bureaucratic matter-of-factness about recent killing.

jokes.text: She put a spell on me! Must... drop... pantaloons.
jokes.punchline: Fake spell compelling him to undress
jokes.explanation: Decent Bart mischief, though the joke is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Ha! Then how come your laundry is always much whiter than mine?
jokes.punchline: Marge cites superior laundry results as evidence of witchcraft
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd evidence for witchcraft - domestic jealousy disguised as supernatural investigation.

jokes.text: Okay, here's how the process works. You sit on the broom and we shove you off the cliff. If you're innocent, you will fall to an honorable Christian death.
jokes.punchline: Death is the outcome for innocence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark logic - trial designed to ensure death regardless of guilt or innocence.

jokes.text: At that point, you will report back here for torture and beheading. Tough, but fair.
jokes.punchline: Homer approves of obviously unfair system
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Homer's approval of rigged system with classic understated delivery.

jokes.text: Doesn't the Bible say, 'Judge not lest ye be judged'?
jokes.punchline: Using scripture against religious persecution
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa intellectualism - trying to use logic and scripture against mob

jokes.text: The Bible says a lot of things. Shove her!
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response followed by immediate violence
jokes.explanation: Good mob mentality - dismissing scripture when inconvenient, then immediate action

jokes.text: Hey, you destroyed my turnip crop. No, that was gophers. Impossible. We burned all the gophers.
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've been burning animals too
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing the town's indiscriminate burning spree - dark absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Impossible. We burned all the gophers.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of ecological genocide
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - shows the extremes of witch hunt mentality applied to everything

jokes.text: This needs more eye of newt. You always want more eye of newt. If it were up to you, the brew would be nothing but newt eyes.
jokes.punchline: Treating supernatural brewing like mundane cooking disagreement
jokes.explanation: Good domestic argument humor applied to witchcraft - familiar sister dynamics in supernatural context.

jokes.text: If I knew you were coming, I'd have baked a cat.
jokes.punchline: Subverts 'baked a cake' with dark witch cooking
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark pun subverting familiar hospitality phrase with witch-appropriate cooking.

jokes.text: His name is Homer.
jokes.punchline: Marge's patient correction
jokes.explanation: Reference to Bewitched TV show running gag - functional but relies on external reference.

jokes.text: We're both 35. We've already gone way past our life expectancy.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to be brave about witch threat
jokes.punchline: Realistic medieval life expectancy as comfort
jokes.explanation: Smart historical humor - using accurate medieval mortality rates as dark optimism.

jokes.text: Hmm. Eat their children. Geez, we were just gonna swipe their shoes. But a good idea is a good idea.
jokes.punchline: Escalate from petty theft to cannibalism based on suggestion
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation humor - casual upgrade from minor mischief to major evil based on peer suggestion.

jokes.text: Are you sure you ladies wouldn't prefer some gingerbread children instead? They're boneless.
jokes.punchline: Maude offering cookie alternative with practical selling point
jokes.explanation: Outstanding dark comedy - treating child cannibalism like a customer service issue with practical product benefits.

jokes.text: You know, scaring people into giving us treats is fun. We should do this every year.
jokes.setup: Witches enjoying their night of terrorizing
jokes.punchline: Inventing Halloween tradition
jokes.explanation: Smart origin story humor - explaining Halloween as organized extortion scheme with annual scheduling.

jokes.text: Yargh. And that's the story of the very first caramel cod. I mean, Halloween.
jokes.setup: Moe concluding story about Halloween origins
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals he was telling wrong story
jokes.explanation: Good storyteller confusion moment - Moe's mix-up reveals he's been telling unrelated story.

jokes.text: Dad, that's our house.
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out they're trick-or-treating at home
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - Lisa's practical correction to Homer's Halloween confusion.
